articles.title: Australia news live: Pocock buys billboards to pressure Chalmers on gas export tax; survey reveals national gloom
articles.summary: 호주 독립 상원의원 데이비드 포콕이 가스 수출에 25% 세금을 부과해 복지와 주택 비용을 마련하자고 주장하고 있습니다. 자선단체와 사회서비스 단체들도 부동산 세금 혜택 축소와 가스 수출 이익 과세를 통해 저소득층을 지원해야 한다고 촉구하고 있습니다. 알바니즈 정부는 다음 달 연방 예산안을 앞두고 강한 압박을 받고 있습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Australian Politician Pushes for Gas Export Tax to Help Vulnerable People

Australian independent senator David Pocock is putting pressure on the government to introduce a new tax on gas exports. He wants energy companies to pay a **25% tax** on the profits they make from selling gas overseas. Pocock has even paid for billboards around the country to spread his message and push Treasurer Jim Chalmers to act.

### Why Is This Tax Being Proposed?

Global energy prices have risen sharply in recent years, partly because of the worldwide energy crisis. As a result, gas companies operating in Australia have made very large profits from selling gas to other countries. Pocock and others argue that ordinary Australians are not benefiting from these profits, while some of the most vulnerable people in the country are struggling badly.

Charities and social service groups support the idea. They say that people on the lowest incomes are already skipping meals, delaying visits to the doctor, and using less heating or electricity just to survive. Frontline services — such as food banks, shelters, and support centres — are completely full and cannot keep up with the growing number of people who need help.

### What Would the Money Be Used For?

Pocock wants the money raised from the gas export tax to fund two main things: **welfare payments** and **housing**. He argues that increasing support payments would help low-income families cover basic costs like food and medicine. Building more affordable housing would also help people who are currently homeless or paying too much rent.

The groups supporting this plan say that the government needs to make "significant and sustained public investment" — meaning large amounts of spending over a long period of time — to truly fix these problems.

### Pressure on Property Tax Rules Too

The Albanese government is also facing calls to change rules around **property investment**. Currently, Australians who invest in property receive two major tax benefits:

- **Negative gearing**: investors can reduce their taxable income if their property costs more than it earns.
- **Capital gains tax discount**: investors only pay tax on 50% of the profit when they sell a property.

Critics say these rules mostly help wealthy investors and make it harder for regular people to buy homes. Charities want the government to reduce or remove these benefits and use the extra tax money to build social housing and increase welfare support.

### What Happens Next?

Australia's federal budget is due next month. This is when the government will announce its spending and tax plans for the coming year. Many people are watching closely to see whether Treasurer Jim Chalmers will take action on gas taxes and property investment rules.

The pressure from Senator Pocock, charities, and community groups shows that many Australians are unhappy with the current situation. A recent survey also revealed a general feeling of pessimism — or "national gloom" — among the Australian public about the direction the country is heading.

Whether the government will respond with bold new policies remains to be seen.

keyVocab.meaning: 수출세 (다른 나라로 물건을 팔 때 내는 세금)
keyVocab.example: The government introduced an export tax on oil to raise money for public services.
Many families rely on welfare payments to cover the cost of food and rent.

keyVocab.meaning: 네거티브 기어링 (부동산 투자 손실로 세금을 줄이는 제도)
keyVocab.example: Negative gearing allows property investors to lower their tax bill if their costs are higher than their rental income.

keyVocab.meaning: 일선 서비스 (사람들을 직접 돕는 복지·지원 기관)
keyVocab.example: Frontline services like food banks and shelters are under great pressure during an economic crisis.

keyVocab.meaning: 지속적인 투자 (장기간에 걸쳐 꾸준히 돈을 쓰는 것)
keyVocab.example: Experts say sustained investment in education is needed to improve outcomes for young people.

articles.title: Blue Origin successfully reused its New Glenn rocket
articles.summary: 블루 오리진의 뉴글렌 로켓이 두 번째 발사에서 성공적으로 착륙해 재사용 가능한 발사체임을 입증했습니다. 하지만 탑재된 AST 스페이스모바일의 위성은 예상보다 낮은 궤도에 진입해 사실상 무용지물이 되었습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Blue Origin Successfully Reuses Its New Glenn Rocket

On April 19, 2026, Blue Origin launched its New Glenn rocket for the second time. The rocket carried a satellite called BlueBird 7, which was built by a company called AST SpaceMobile. The launch had both good news and bad news.

### The Good News: A Reusable Rocket

The most exciting part of the mission was the successful landing of the rocket's first stage booster. After lifting off, the booster returned to Earth and landed safely on its landing pad. This was the second time the same booster had launched and landed, which means it is officially a **reusable** rocket.

This is a big achievement for Blue Origin and its founder, Jeff Bezos. A reusable rocket is important because it can be used again for future missions, which saves a lot of money compared to throwing away a rocket after just one use. Blue Origin's competitor, SpaceX, has been doing this successfully with its Falcon 9 rocket for many years. Now, Blue Origin has joined that group.

### The Bad News: The Satellite Problem

Unfortunately, not everything went perfectly. The mission's main goal was to deliver AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite into the correct orbit around Earth. However, the second stage of the rocket — the upper part that carries the satellite into space — did not perform as planned. It placed the satellite into a **lower orbit** than expected.

This is a serious problem. A satellite in the wrong orbit cannot do its job properly. AST SpaceMobile confirmed that the satellite did separate from the rocket and turn on, but because it is in the wrong position in space, it is essentially useless. The company has not yet said whether it can fix the problem or whether the satellite is a total loss.

### What Is AST SpaceMobile?

AST SpaceMobile is a company that is trying to build a network of satellites that work like cell towers in space. The idea is to allow regular smartphones to connect to the internet from anywhere on Earth — even in remote areas where there are no traditional cell towers. Each BlueBird satellite is a key part of this plan, so losing one is a significant setback for the company.

### What Happens Next?

Despite the satellite problem, Blue Origin can celebrate the successful recovery of the New Glenn booster. Engineers will now inspect the booster and prepare it for a third flight. This proves that New Glenn is a real competitor in the growing market for reusable rockets.

For AST SpaceMobile, the situation is more difficult. The company will need to investigate what went wrong with the second stage and decide how to move forward with its satellite network plans.

keyVocab.example: A reusable rocket can fly multiple times, saving the company a lot of money.
The booster separated from the rocket after liftoff and returned safely to the ground.
The satellite was placed into the wrong orbit, so it could not work correctly.
Losing the satellite was a major setback for the company's plans.
The new satellite network will help people living in remote areas connect to the internet.

articles.title: Ella Langley’s ‘Dandelion’ Debuts at No. 1 on Album Chart, Following a Six-Week Run by ‘Choosin’ Texas’ Atop the Hot 100
articles.summary: 엘라 랭글리의 앨범 'Dandelion'이 빌보드 앨범 차트에서 1위로 데뷔했습니다. 이는 그녀의 싱글 'Choosin' Texas'가 빌보드 핫 100 차트에서 6주 연속 1위를 차지한 이후의 성과입니다. 2026년 엘라 랭글리는 싱글과 앨범 모두에서 큰 성공을 거두고 있습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Ella Langley's 'Dandelion' Debuts at No. 1 on the Album Chart

Country singer Ella Langley is having an incredible year in 2026. She is not just successful with one song — she is winning on multiple charts at the same time.

### 'Choosin' Texas' Dominates the Hot 100

Earlier this year, Ella Langley's hit single "Choosin' Texas" spent six weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100. The Billboard Hot 100 is one of the most important music charts in the United States. It tracks the most popular songs across all music genres each week. Spending six weeks at the top is a remarkable achievement, and it shows just how popular Ella Langley has become.

### A New Album Enters at the Top

Now, Ella Langley has added another major success to her 2026 story. Her album, called "Dandelion," debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Album Chart. "Debuted" means it entered the chart for the first time — and it went straight to the top position. This is called a "No. 1 debut," and it is something that not many artists can achieve.

The success of "Dandelion" is closely connected to the popularity of "Choosin' Texas." When a single becomes very popular, many fans become curious about the artist and want to hear more of their music. This often leads people to buy or stream the artist's full album. This is exactly what happened with Ella Langley. The huge success of "Choosin' Texas" brought many new listeners to her album.

### More Than a 'Singles Artist'

Some musicians are known mainly for one or two popular singles, but their albums do not sell as well. These artists are sometimes called "singles artists." However, Ella Langley has proven that she is much more than that. Both her single and her album have reached No. 1, which shows that fans enjoy her music as a whole, not just one hit song.

### Who Is Ella Langley?

Ella Langley is a country music singer and songwriter. She has been building her career over the past few years and has gained a growing fanbase thanks to her honest lyrics and strong vocals. "Choosin' Texas" helped introduce her to a much wider audience, including many listeners who do not usually follow country music.

### What This Means for Her Career

Having both a No. 1 single and a No. 1 album in the same year is a very big deal in the music industry. It shows strong commercial success and wide public interest. For Ella Langley, 2026 is shaping up to be a defining year — one that could establish her as one of the biggest names in country music for years to come.

Fans and music journalists will be watching closely to see how long "Dandelion" stays at the top of the chart and what Ella Langley does next.

keyVocab.example: The singer's new album debuted at No. 1 on the chart.
Winning two awards in one night was a great achievement for the young actor.
The ubiquity of the hit song meant you could hear it on every radio station.
The band built a large fanbase by touring across the country.
The film was a commercial success, earning millions of dollars in its first week.

articles.title: This Is a Hard Time to Start a Career. These Two Words Can Help.
articles.summary: 현재 취업 시장은 매우 어렵지만, 두 가지 핵심 단어를 활용하면 보람 있는 직업 생활을 만들어 나갈 수 있습니다. 경력을 시작하는 사람들을 위한 실용적인 조언을 담고 있습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Starting a Career in Difficult Times

Looking for a job right now is not easy. Many young people are entering the workforce during a period of economic uncertainty, limited job openings, and strong competition. It can feel discouraging, but career experts say there are strategies that can help.

### Why Is It So Hard?

The job market today presents several challenges. Companies are being more careful about hiring new employees. Some industries have slowed down, and technology is changing the kinds of jobs that are available. At the same time, many people are applying for the same positions, making it harder to stand out.

For recent graduates or people changing careers, this situation can feel overwhelming. You may send out many applications and hear nothing back. You might feel like your skills are not good enough, or that you chose the wrong field. These feelings are very common.

### Two Words That Can Help

Career advisors often point to two important words when it comes to building a successful work life: **"genuine curiosity."**

Genuine curiosity means showing a real, honest interest in the work you do and the people around you. It is not about pretending to be excited. It is about asking good questions, learning from others, and being open to new experiences.

When you approach your job search — and your actual job — with genuine curiosity, several good things can happen:

- You become more engaged and motivated, even when the work is difficult.
- You ask better questions during interviews, which impresses employers.
- You learn faster than people who simply follow instructions without thinking.
- You build stronger relationships with colleagues and mentors.

### How to Practice Genuine Curiosity

Here are some practical ways to develop this habit:

**Ask questions often.** When you meet someone in your field, ask them about their career path. How did they get started? What do they wish they had known earlier? Most people enjoy sharing their experiences.

**Stay informed.** Read news and articles about your industry. Understanding the bigger picture makes you a more valuable employee and gives you interesting topics to discuss.

**Try new things.** Take on projects that are slightly outside your comfort zone. Volunteer for tasks that help you learn new skills, even if they are not directly related to your current role.

**Reflect on what you learn.** After a meeting, a project, or even a rejection, ask yourself: "What did I learn from this?" This habit turns every experience into an opportunity for growth.

### Building a Rewarding Work Life

A rewarding career is not built overnight. It takes time, patience, and a willingness to keep learning. The job market may be tough right now, but people who show genuine curiosity tend to find their way forward.

Employers notice people who are truly interested in their work. Even in a competitive market, standing out does not always require the most impressive qualifications. Sometimes, it simply requires showing that you care — about the work, about the company, and about your own growth.

If you are starting your career right now, remember: the difficulty you face today is building the resilience you will need tomorrow.

keyVocab.example: Many workers feel anxiety because of economic uncertainty.
Applying for dozens of jobs at once can feel overwhelming.
She showed genuine interest in learning about the company's goals.
Facing rejection many times helped him build resilience in his career.
He did not have all the qualifications, but his curiosity impressed the interviewer.

articles.title: Fake ‘Retreat,’ Real Product: The Hot Sauce in ‘Jury Duty: Company Retreat’ Is Now Available to Buy Online
articles.summary: 아마존 리얼리티 프로그램 'Jury Duty' 시즌 2에서 주인공 앤서니 노먼은 가짜 가족 핫소스 회사를 위해 일하는 것으로 설정되었습니다. 프로그램이 끝난 후, 쇼에 등장했던 그 핫소스가 실제로 온라인에서 판매되기 시작했습니다. 가상의 회사 'Rockin' Grandmas'가 실제 제품으로 이어진 것입니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## The Hot Sauce from 'Jury Duty: Company Retreat' Is Now Real — and You Can Buy It

Sometimes, something that starts as fiction becomes reality. That is exactly what happened with the hot sauce featured in the second season of Amazon's popular reality series, "Jury Duty."

### What Is 'Jury Duty'?

"Jury Duty" is a social experiment series on Amazon. In the show, one real person — who does not know they are being filmed — is placed in a situation with actors. Hidden cameras record everything. In the first season, the unsuspecting star was a man named Ronald Gladden, who thought he was serving on a real jury. The show became very popular because viewers enjoyed watching someone react honestly to unusual and funny situations.

### Season 2: A Fake Company Retreat

The second season of "Jury Duty," subtitled "Company Retreat," followed a similar idea. This time, the unwitting star was a man named Anthony Norman. Anthony believed he was attending a real company retreat — a kind of team-building trip organized by his employer. However, everything around him was carefully planned and performed by actors.

The company Anthony thought he worked for was a family-owned hot sauce business called "Rockin' Grandmas." Of course, this company did not actually exist. It was completely made up for the show. The actors created a believable world around Anthony, including a fictional product — the "Rockin' Grandmas" hot sauce.

### From Fiction to Reality

The second season of "Jury Duty" finished airing this month, and at the end of the show, Anthony found out the truth — that everything had been a set-up. However, the story did not end there.

In a surprising and fun twist, the hot sauce that was invented for the show is now a real product. Fans of "Jury Duty" can now actually buy "Rockin' Grandmas" hot sauce online. What began as a fictional prop used in a TV show has become a genuine item available for purchase.

### Why Is This Interesting?

This kind of crossover — where something from a TV show becomes a real product — is becoming more common in the entertainment industry. It is a clever way to keep fans engaged even after a show ends. Buying the hot sauce lets fans feel connected to the show and its story.

For Anthony Norman, the experience was likely both surprising and amusing. He went through an entire fake work event without knowing it was staged, and now the fake product from that experience is something anyone in the world can buy.

### The Popularity of 'Jury Duty'

The "Jury Duty" series has been praised for its creative format and genuinely funny moments. Unlike traditional reality TV, which often involves competition or conflict, "Jury Duty" is built around kindness and humor. Audiences have responded very positively to this approach, making it one of Amazon's more talked-about original programs.

Whether or not the hot sauce itself tastes good, it has already become a memorable piece of pop culture — a small bottle that reminds fans of a show that blurred the line between fiction and real life.

keyVocab.example: The unwitting participant had no idea he was part of a hidden camera show.
The company in the story was fictitious and did not exist in the real world.
The TV show was a social experiment designed to test how people behave under pressure.
The sword used in the movie was just a prop made of plastic.
The crossover between the TV show and a real product surprised many fans.
